methods.description: Process for collecting and preparing sediment cores using a Livingstone piston corer for various analytical approaches.

steps.description: Sampling was carried out with the help of a Livingstone piston corer, following the so-called 'overlapping' method.
steps.searchable_summary: collect sediment cores using Livingstone piston corer with overlapping method

steps.description: Cores were halved lengthwise after having been transported to the lab, to suit the needs of different analytical approaches.
steps.searchable_summary: halve sediment cores lengthwise for analysis

methods.description: Radiocarbon measurements and analysis of samples from peat layers, charcoals, and flue-ash horizons to determine lithostratigraphy and chronological units.

steps.description: Radiocarbon measurements were carried out on 7 samples derived from the peat layers, the inwashed charcoals and flue-ash horizons in the Light Isotope Laboratory of the Nuclear Research Center at the Hungarian Academy of Sciences in Debrecen.
steps.searchable_summary: perform radiocarbon measurements on samples in isotope laboratory

steps.description: The methodology and techniques presented in Hertelendi et al. (1989) were followed for the digestion and measurements.
steps.searchable_summary: apply Hertelendi et al. 1989 techniques for sample digestion and measurement

steps.description: These 3 samples were analyzed with AMS in the Isotope Laboratory of Oxford University (Willis 1997).
steps.searchable_summary: analyze samples with AMS at Oxford University

steps.description: Radiocarbon dates were utilized to determine the lithostratigraphy and chronological units with the help of the results of geochemical, pollen analytical and malacological analyses.
steps.searchable_summary: use radiocarbon dates to determine lithostratigraphy and chronological units

methods.description: Utilization of pollen analysis results for comparative biostratigraphy and paleo-environment reconstruction.

steps.description: The results of pollen analysis were used as input in comparative biostratigraphy.
steps.searchable_summary: use pollen analysis results for biostratigraphy

steps.description: In order to gain an expansive picture of the paleo-environment, pollen data published earlier by Katherine Jane Willis (Willis 1997) have been utilized.
steps.searchable_summary: use published pollen data for paleo-environment analysis

methods.description: Analysis of mollusk samples from sediment layers to determine paleo-ecological groups and environmental conditions.

steps.description: Samples collected from layers between 8-12cm were washed through a mesh screen for mollusks.
steps.searchable_summary: wash sediment samples through mesh screen to collect mollusks

steps.description: The work of Sümegi and Krolopp (2002), Meijer (1985), Lo∫ek (1964) was utilized to determine and put the species into paleo-ecological groups according to their temperature tolerance, recent biogeographic affinity and distribution, as well as their humidity and vegetation cover preferences.
steps.searchable_summary: classify mollusk species into paleo-ecological groups

steps.description: Both the dominance values of the species found and the ratio of paleo-ecological and recent biogeographical groups, along with the results of the radiocarbon measurements and the lithostratigraphy, were depicted on graphs with Psimpoll software (Bennett 1992).
steps.searchable_summary: visualize species dominance and ecological group ratios using Psimpoll
